evals.prompt: let's figure out my morning routine
evals.expected_output: A new workflows/*.md file (e.g. workflows/morning-routine.md) capturing the morning-routine loop as a finished spec: an explicit Trigger, any Checkpoints with a stated Brief, and no open question. The session gets there by asking one question at a time, each with a recommended answer, and reaches for the Trigger/Checkpoint/Push right/Brief vocabulary only where the loop actually calls for it — never as a mandatory checklist.
assertions: A workflows/*.md file is created (not just discussed in chat)
The session asks one question at a time, not a batch of questions
Each question posed has a recommended answer attached
The resulting spec states an explicit Trigger (an event or a schedule)
Every Checkpoint in the spec has a stated Brief
No open question, TBD, or placeholder remains in the finished spec

evals.prompt: Revise workflows/inbox-triage.md — it doesn't say who gets pinged when an urgent bug report comes in, or what the checkpoint actually shows the reviewer.
evals.expected_output: workflows/inbox-triage.md is edited in place (not replaced by a new file) to close the two named gaps: an explicit escalation/notification step for urgent bug reports, and a stated Brief for the existing checkpoint. The rest of the spec's structure is preserved unless the grilling surfaces a reason to change it.
assertions: The existing workflows/inbox-triage.md file is updated rather than a new file being created
The revised spec states who or what gets pinged for an urgent bug report
Every Checkpoint in the revised spec has a stated Brief
No open question remains in the revised spec
Parts of the original spec unrelated to the two named gaps are preserved rather than rewritten wholesale

evals.prompt: Can you help me set up a workflow for how our team handles incoming Slack requests?
evals.expected_output: Because NOTES.md is empty, the session first interviews the user about their world — the tools they use, the channels they process, and their own terms for both — recording sharpened, canonical terms into NOTES.md before proposing any workflow structure. Only after NOTES.md has real content does the session move on to grilling toward a finished workflows/*.md spec, using the terminology just recorded.
assertions: The session interviews the user about their tools, channels, and terminology before proposing a workflow structure
NOTES.md is updated with the user's world and canonical terms before workflows/*.md is created or edited
Terms recorded in NOTES.md are reused consistently in the resulting workflow spec
The final workflow spec contains no open question
Every Checkpoint in the final spec, if any, has a stated Brief
